content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 12, 2013
William C. Bradshaw
295 Main Street, Suite 300
Salinas, CA 93901

Re: Your Request for General Information
Our File No. G-13-113

Dear Mr. Bradshaw:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ It does not appear from the limited information you provided that you are authorized by the named public officials to request advice. Regulation 18329, copy enclose, provides that the Commission may decline to provide advice where the request is submitted by a third party about another person's duties under the Act.

However, I did want to direct you to the following general information that may relate to your questions:

1. Prohibition on Personal Use of Campaign Funds.
Government Code Sections 89510 - 89522

2. Prohibition on Use of State Property for Campaign Purposes.
Penal Code Section 424
http://oag.ca.gov/ethics/accessible/misuse

Should you have any additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

JWW:jg
Enclosure

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked for advice regarding the Political Reform Act, but the FPPC determined it lacked authorization from the named public officials to provide advice on their duties.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the request was submitted by a third party about another person's duties under the Act, citing Regulation 18329. However, the FPPC provided general information on the prohibition of personal use of campaign funds and misuse of state property.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination letter with general information provided rather than specific advice. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; content extracted from letter body. Limited details about the original inquiry provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor asked for advice regarding the Political Reform Act, but the FPPC determined it lacked authorization from the named public officials to provide advice on their duties.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the request was submitted by a third party about another person's duties under the Act, citing Regulation 18329. However, the FPPC provided general information on the prohibition of personal use of campaign funds and misuse of state property.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined Mr. Bradshaw's request for advice under Regulation 18329 since he is a third party seeking advice about other officials' duties, but directed him to relevant statutes regarding campaign fund use and state property misuse.
